<!-- Topic 4: Programming Activity -->
<!-- Total slides: 2 -->

# Code Together

---

## Programming Exercise {.smaller}

Create a **dynamic array** for pet ages using the `short` type. Start with 1 element and populate it.

+ Ask the user how many new ages to **add**. Grow the dynamic array and populate the new elements.
+ Ask the user how many ages to **delete**. Shrink the dynamic array by that count.
+ Adding appends to the **right end** of the array; deleting removes from the **right end** — for this exercise only. In reality you can add or delete anywhere.
+ **Print** the full array after each activity: after initial population, after adding, after deleting.

::: notes
Total slides: 2
Give students ~12–15 minutes. They have all the pieces: new[], delete[], the 6-step resize algorithm. The challenge is applying it cleanly twice in a row (grow, then shrink) while managing the size variable consistently throughout.

Remind: use short, not int. Remind: the resize algorithm is exactly the same each time — only the direction (++ vs --) and copy logic changes.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## Requirements checklist {.smaller}

- [ ] Declare `short* ages = nullptr` and `int size = 1`
- [ ] Allocate initial array with `new short[size]`, populate, and print
- [ ] Ask user how many to add → apply the 6-step increase algorithm → populate new slots → print
- [ ] Ask user how many to delete → apply the 6-step decrease algorithm → print
- [ ] Each resize uses the same pattern: create temp → copy → `delete[]` old → swing pointer → null temp
- [ ] Call `delete[] ages` and set to `nullptr` before `return 0`

<!-- Slide 2 -->
